subject: UX & Research
title: The Samsung Galaxy Note 7 Recall
description: A 60-second lesson on The Samsung Galaxy Note 7 Recall.

hook.text: Samsung's Galaxy Note 7 caught fire. Twice. Even the replacements exploded. Airlines banned them.

buildup.text: Batteries were physically too large for the case. Internal pressure caused thermal runaway and fires.

discovery.text: Samsung issued a recall and sent fireproof shipping boxes. Users had to return phones in safety containers.

twist.text: The recall UX was terrible. Confusing exchange processes and unclear instructions delayed returns for weeks.

climax.text: Crisis UX matters. When things go wrong, the recovery experience defines whether users stay or leave forever.

punchline.text: When things break, the recovery experience is your product.

quiz.question: What UX lesson came from Samsung's Note 7 crisis?
options: The recovery and recall experience is as important as the product itself
Batteries should never be used in phones
Users don't care about safety features
